Aria II Reference Guide 

Start Up 

Area Prep 

Turn on BioBubble  

Clean instrument 

 Get tubes ready 

Bleach 

Water 

Nozzle 

Turn on cytometer 

 Check bubble filter 

 Power on Aria 

 Log into software 

Stream  

 Clean sorting chamber 

Nozzle holder 

Flow cell area 

Plates 

 Nozzle 

Sonicate if more than 1 day since last use 

Make sure software is in correct configuration 

Place prepped nozzle in flow cell 

 Turn on stream 

Check stream position in waste drawer 

Focus stream light 

QC Beads 

Prep for Beads 

 Clean Flow Cell 

Bleach 

Ideally 20-30 min 

Flow Rate = 11 

Water 

Ideally 20-30 min 

Flow Rate = 11 

 Set Sweet Spot 

Adjust amplitude 

4.5 < amplitude value < 20.0 

Droplet and satellite come back together by bottom line 

If values are outside: 

Sonicate nozzle and try again 

If issue is still present, will need to change frequency à Grab Bryan/Dr. Fan for this 

Set gap number to match current value 

Change drop 1 to match value 

Set “Sweet Spot” to on 

CST Beads 

 Load CST Beads 

Make sure on correct QC template 

Flow Rate = 1.0 

 Run Beads 

Change window extension 

Record 

 Unload Beads 

Accudrop Beads 

 Load Beads 

Make sure event rate is above 800 

Flow rate should not be above 2.0 

 Prep to Run Auto Delay 

Open sorting layout 

Tube 

Precision is set to “Fine Tune” 

Start sort 

Set side streams 

Inner left side stream is set to ~ 21 

All other side streams at 0 

Turn on Optical Filter 

Adjust inner left side stream 

Should be just left of center in left-hand box 

Values add up to 100.0 

Run Auto Delay 

Make sure displayed figure looks normal 

Setting Up for Sort in Tubes 

 Pick tube holder 

 Run Test Sort 

 Adjust all streams, as needed 

5 mL Tube Holder 

Inner tubes 

Just left of center 

Outer tubes 

Just right of center 

15 mL Tube Holder 

Just left of center 

 Wash cytometer 

Bleach 

Water 

Setting Up for Plate Sort 

 General Notes 

100 µm nozzle only 

Flow rate = 1.0 

Evts/sec ≤ 1,000 

Sorting Precision = Single Cell 

 Plate Adapter and Side Stream Adjustment 

Clean plate adapter with EtOH 

Insert adapter into sorting chamber 

Adjust the side stream position 

Close all but far left side stream 

Adjust stream 

Just left of the waste catch 

Stream falls through the hole in the plate adapter 

Turn on plate voltage 

 Adjust Plate Positioning 

Initial position assessment 

Sort à Home Device 

Select the correct plate that you will be using for the sort 

Typically, will be “Falcon 96-well” 

Test the current plate position 

Click the test sort button on the plate window 

Look at the position of the droplet within the A1 well 

Adjust plate position and re-test 

Based on the placement of the initial test sort above, adjust the plate position 

Re-test with the test sort 

Repeat steps as necessary until droplet is in the center of the well 

Save the position 

Click “Set Home” 

Close out window 

 Test Sort 

Open plate sort layout 

Not P2 Gate 

15 events per well 

At least ½ of rows and columns selected for sorting 

Run the plate sort with Accudrop beads 

Remove plate from holder and evaluate 

All sorted droplets should be at center of well (or close) 

Adjust the plate positioning if needed 

Experiment Template Prep (can be done at any time) 

Importing from Previous Run 

 Import most recent experiment w/ same panel 

 Duplicate without data 

 Change dates as necessary 

New Panel 

 Import most recent experiment user ran 

 Add Correct Detectors 

Parameters Tab 

Remove Unneeded Detectors 

Add Detectors 

All detectors that match dyes on sorting form 

Leave one open detector 

Change Detector Labels 

Experiment Layout 

Label with marker and fluorochrome  

 Remove any Compensation 

 Set Up Compensation Matrix (if needed) 

Shortest à Longest λ 

Do not include live/dead 

 Readjust Gating Plots 

Delete any unnecessary gates 

Make sure plots are displaying correct populations 

 Delete all but 1 tube 

Totally New Experiment 

 Create new experiment 

Experiment à Experiment Template à Blank experiment w/ sample tube 

 Relabel Experiment 

Name 

Year-MMDD_UserInitials_ 

Specimen 

Year-MMDD 

 Adjust Detectors 

Make sure proper parameters are being recorded 

FSC and SSC 

Linear 

A 

H 

W 

Colors 

Log 

A 

See III B 2 for further information 

 Create Plots 

Population Hierarchy 

Populations à Show Population Hierarchy 

Scatters Plot 

FSC-A vs SSC-A 

Singlets Plot 

FSC-H vs FSC-W 

Area Scaling Plot 

FSC-H vs FSC-A 

Compensation Matrix 

If needed. 

See III B 4 

Any potential gating plots 

 Prepare Template for Run 

Change voltages for FSC and SSC 

1 ≤ FSC ≤ 100 

250 ≤ SSC ≤ 350 

Record a few events to save voltages 

Change the stopping gate to singlets 

Running Sort 

Gate Set-Up 

 Cell Suspension Prep 

Check to see if cells need to be filtered 

Make sure event rate is below threshold for nozzle 

Event Thresholdnozzle= Diameternozzle4
E
v
e
n
t
 
T
h
r
e
s
h
o
l
d
n
o
z
z
l
e
=
 
D
i
a
m
e
t
e
r
n
o
z
z
l
e
4
 
    

If event rate ≥ event threshold 

Check flow rate is at 1.0 

Dilute suspension, if necessary 

 Set Voltages 

Use an all-color sample 

Make sure all plots are not on biexponential  

Alter Values 

Adjust area scaling 

Adjust scatters 

Fine-tune area scaling 

Adjust color voltages 

Record ~ 5k events 

 Compensation (if needed) 

Manual Compensation 

Convert scale on matrix plots to biexponential 

Run and record all single colors 

~1k events for beads 

May need to adjust scatter values 

~5k events for cells 

Adjust Compensation 

Change scatters gate 

Shrink to population of interest 

Color by color 

Copy spectral overlap 

Paste spectral overlap w/ zeroes on next tube 

Check Compensation 

Paste final compensation to each tube 

Check all single colors again 

Paste acceptable compensation 

Already recorded sample tubes 

Global settings 

Automatic Compensation 

Set-Up 

“Create Compensation Controls” 

Delete Unnecessary Values 

Labeled detectors 

Live/dead detector 

Un-used detector 

Switch to compensation worksheet 

Record Single Colors 

Calculate Compensation 

“Link & Save” 

Change back to experiment worksheet 

Check Compensation 

Use compensation matrix 

Adjust values as necessary 

Follow procedures in Manual Compensation sections iii – iv. 

 Gates 

Adjust pre-drawn gates as needed 

If new gate is needed: 

Click on correct parent gate in population hierarchy 

Draw gate on correct plot 

Make sure populations shown are valid 

Draw gate 

 Run Additional Controls (if needed) 

 Clarify Sorting Needs 

Determine populations to sort 

Determine cell numbers needed 

Min/Max 

See what cells will be used for 

Timing for samples 

Preferred contact method 

 Set Up Sorting Layout 

Device Type 

Adjust all values as determined in section 5 

Sorting 

 Prior to sorting start 

Tubes 

Make sure collection tubes are in holder 

Verify tubes match sorting layout positions 

Keep tube caps sterile 

Put holder in plastic container with ice packs 

Sample 

Double check cell concentration 

See IVA1bi 

 During sort 

Note: All steps should be done throughout the entire sorting process 

Ensure that sample line is not clogging 

Event rate should not be jumping drastically 

FSC and SSC should look same as start of run 

Stream should be stable 

Image looks stable 

Amplitude is stable 

Record full amount of events 

Watch collection tubes 

Approximate cell counts for 85 µm nozzle 

Microcentrifuge =  

5 mL tube = 1.5x106 

15 mL tube = 6.0x106 

Approximate cell counts for 100 µm nozzle 

Microcentrifuge =  

5 mL tube = 1.0x106 

15 mL tube = 3.0x106 

Watch sample tube 

Make sure not running out of volume 

Filter as needed 

Evaluate sort  

Flow rate ≤ 4.0 

Efficiency ≥ 85% 

Check sorting rate 

[Equation] 

Cell countstart = Cell number right before “Restart” is hit 

Cell countend = Cell number right as “Elapsed Time” hits 00:01:00 

Change rate to match desired evaluation period 

Cells/hr 

Used to determine if able to hit desired cell number within allotted time 

[Equation] 

Cells/min  

Can give a better idea of when a tube will need to be changed 

[Equation] 

Communicate with sort user 

If rate calculations indicate cell number will not hit desired value in allotted time 

If you know desired cell count cannot be reached with sample given 

When samples are done 

Any other concerns as needed 

Post Sorting 

 Run Purity check, if needed 

Make sure to wash with water for at least 1 min prior to running 

Event rate should show zero consistently before running purity 

Record ~ 2k events 

 Export 

Experiment 

Figures 

Sort Reports 

 Clean Instrument 

Bleach 

Water 

 Admin Tasks 

Upload data to SharePoint 

Update sorting time on iLabs 

Delete experiment from FACSDiva once a&b completed 

Daily Shutdown 

Wash Cytometer 

 Bleach (~10 min) 

 Water (~5 min) 

Turn Off Stream 

Clean Flow Cell 

Use water as “cleaning agent” 

Remove Nozzle 

Empty Waste 

Turn off Aria 

 Software 

 Cytometer 

 Computer 

 bioBubble 

Cytometer Maintenance 

Weekly Start Up 

 Empty EtOH 

Drain EtOH from filter 

Pour EtOH from sheath tank back into EtOH container 

Rinse tank with water ≥ 2 times 

 Flush System with Water 

Fill sheath tank with water 

Connect tank to Aria 

Filter 

Air Pressure 

Circular Connector 

Run Fluidics Start Up 

Possible Nozzle Choices: 

Closed Loop 

Bad 130 µm 

Bleed Filter of Air 

After loud clicks 

Use top port 

Rinse Filter with Water 

Use bottom port 

Fill the funnel bucket once 

Repeat at least 1 more time 

Drain Sheath Tank 

Remove Connectors 

Dump Water in Sink 

 Flush System with Sheath 

Make Sheath 

Can be done during water steps above 

10X stock solution à 1X in Tank 

Use Sterile Bottles 

Rinse Tank with Water ≥ 2 Times 

Add 1X Sheath to Tank 

Reconnect Tank to Aria 

Run Fluidics Start Up 

Bleed Filter of Air 

Use Same Nozzle as Water 

Remove nozzle and get ready for set up 

Weekly Shut Down 

 Disconnect tank from fluidics cart 

 Drain filter of sheath  

Add EtOH to Tank 

Dump Remaining Sheath in Sink 

Rinse with Water ≥ 2 times 

Add EtOH  

Reconnect to Fluidics Cart 

 Run Fluidics Start Up 

Possible Nozzle Choices: 

Closed Loop 

Bad 130 µm 

Bleed Filter of Air 

Note: This is only needed for 1st Fluidics Start Up 

After loud clicks 

Use top port 

 Repeat ≥ 1 time 

 Add “Ethanol” label to sheath tank  

Turn off cytometer 

Bleach Clean (In Progress) 

 When to run 

Quarterly 

After several contamination events 

 Run Bleach  

Disconnect tank from Fluidics Cart 

Remove filter from fluidics line 

Drain filter of sheath 

 Make bleach 

5% Bleach 

≤ 5 L 

Add bleach to tank 

Reconnect tank to Fluidics Cart 

Make sure filter is NOT connected to tank 

 Run Fluidics Start Up 

Use bad 130 µm nozzle 

 Turn on stream 

 Run bleach tube for ≥ 1 hour 

Turn off stream 

Disconnect tank from Fluidics Cart 

Dump bleach in sink 

 Clean Sheath Tank 

Rinse tank with water and dump 

Full Tank Rinse 

Fill tank with water 

Close Tank with Lid 

Roll water around ENTIRE tank 

Dump water 

Repeat ≥ 2 times 

 Run Water 

1st System Rinse 

Add water to tank 

Connect tank to Fluidics Cart 

Filter is NOT attached to fluidics line 

Run Fluidics Start Up 

Turn on stream 

Run water tube ≥ 30 min 

Turn off stream 

Disconnect tank from Fluidics Cart 

2nd System Rinse 

Attach filter to fluidics line 

Repeat steps a iii- a v 

Bleed filter of air during fluidics start up 

Leave system in water if done before break 

Otherwise run a sheath start up
